title: A stairwell painted by my neighbour in Antwerp
summary: A short story about a neighbour who quietly painted a shared space in your building.
topic: A stairwell painted by my neighbour in Antwerp
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a small improvement a neighbour made to a shared space in your building. Describe the before and after, and what you said to them. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: For a year and a half, the stairwell in our building in Antwerp was…
Then one Saturday morning, I came home and found…
I knocked on the door of apartment three and asked…
sampleText: For a year and a half, the stairwell in our building in Antwerp was the saddest colour I have ever seen — a kind of greenish grey that made everyone look tired in the morning. Three light bulbs were broken. Nobody complained, because nobody knew whose job it was. Then one Saturday morning in October, I came home from the market and found a tall woman from apartment three standing on a borrowed ladder, painting the wall pale yellow with a small brush. She had already finished half of the second floor. She was wearing old jeans and humming something I did not recognise. I stood at the bottom of the stairs for a moment, holding my bag of vegetables, watching her work. When she noticed me, she smiled and said, 'I asked the landlord. He didn't say no.' I went upstairs, put down my bag, and came back with a second brush. We painted together for the rest of the morning. By Monday the whole stairwell smelled faintly of fresh paint, and the new tenants kept stopping on the landings to look up. Nobody knows who put the small jug of flowers on the window at the third floor a week later. But everyone knows who started it.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the stairwell in our building in Antwerp was the saddest colour
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: giếng cầu thang trong tòa nhà chúng tôi ở Antwerp là màu buồn nhất

sampleAnnotations.phrase: nobody knew whose job it was
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không ai biết đó là việc của ai

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một người phụ nữ cao từ căn hộ số ba đang đứng trên một chiếc thang mượn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: painting the wall pale yellow with a small brush
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đang sơn tường màu vàng nhạt bằng một chiếc cọ nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: She had already finished half of the second floor
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cô ấy đã sơn xong một nửa tầng hai

sampleAnnotations.phrase: She was wearing old jeans and humming something
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cô ấy đang mặc quần bò cũ và ngân nga điều gì đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I asked the landlord. He didn't say no
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đã hỏi chủ nhà. Ông ấy không nói không

sampleAnnotations.phrase: We painted together for the rest of the morning
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Chúng tôi cùng sơn cho hết buổi sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the new tenants kept stopping on the landings to look up

mọi người đều biết ai đã khởi đầu nó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for prior progress
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'She had already finished half of the second floor' dùng past perfect ('had finished') để chỉ một hành động hoàn thành trước thời điểm tôi nhìn thấy (past simple 'found').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'She had already finished half of the second floor' uses past perfect ('had finished') for an action completed before the past moment I saw it ('found').

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for vivid scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'painting the wall pale yellow', 'was wearing old jeans', 'humming something' — past continuous (hoặc hiện tại phân từ rút gọn) tạo cảm giác sống động cho khung cảnh.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Painting the wall pale yellow', 'was wearing old jeans', 'humming something' — past continuous (or reduced present participles) bring the scene to life.

sampleTranslationVi: Suốt một năm rưỡi, giếng cầu thang trong tòa nhà chúng tôi ở Antwerp là màu buồn nhất tôi từng thấy — một thứ xám hơi xanh khiến mọi người trông mệt mỏi vào buổi sáng. Ba bóng đèn bị hỏng. Không ai phàn nàn, vì không ai biết đó là việc của ai. Rồi một sáng thứ Bảy tháng Mười, tôi về từ chợ và thấy một người phụ nữ cao từ căn hộ số ba đang đứng trên một chiếc thang mượn, đang sơn tường màu vàng nhạt bằng một chiếc cọ nhỏ. Cô ấy đã sơn xong một nửa tầng hai. Cô ấy đang mặc quần bò cũ và ngân nga điều gì đó tôi không nhận ra. Tôi đứng dưới chân cầu thang một lát, cầm túi rau, nhìn cô ấy làm việc. Khi cô ấy thấy tôi, cô mỉm cười và nói, 'Tôi đã hỏi chủ nhà. Ông ấy không nói không.' Tôi đi lên, đặt túi xuống, và quay lại với một chiếc cọ thứ hai. Chúng tôi cùng sơn cho hết buổi sáng. Đến thứ Hai cả giếng cầu thang thoang thoảng mùi sơn mới, và những người thuê mới cứ dừng lại ở các chiếu nghỉ để nhìn lên. Không ai biết ai đã đặt chiếc bình nhỏ cắm hoa lên cửa sổ tầng ba một tuần sau đó. Nhưng mọi người đều biết ai đã khởi đầu nó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence about duration.
options: For a year and a half, the stairwell is the saddest colour I have ever seen.
For a year and a half, the stairwell was the saddest colour I have ever seen.
For a year and a half, the stairwell was the most sad colour I have ever seen.
For a year and a half, the stairwell were the saddest colour I have ever seen.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'was' for a finished past state; superlative 'saddest' (short adjective + -est).

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past continuous sentence.
options: She painting the wall pale yellow with a small brush.
She paint the wall pale yellow with a small brush.
She was painting the wall pale yellow with a small brush.
She is painted the wall pale yellow with a small brush.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was painting' for an action in progress at a past moment.

options: a kind of doorbell
a window between two floors
the front door of a building
the vertical space in a building that contains the stairs
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stairwell' = the shaft / vertical space holding the staircase.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past perfect sentence.
options: She had already finished half of the second floor.
She has already finished half of the second floor.
She was already finished half of the second floor.
She had already finish half of the second floor.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had finished' for an action completed before another past event.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct reported speech / direct quote sentence.
options: She smiled and said, 'I have asked the landlord. He doesn't say no.'
She smiled and said, 'I asked the landlord. He didn't say no.'
She smiled and said, 'I asking the landlord. He didn't say no.'
She smiled and said that, 'I asked the landlord.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Direct quotation; past simple inside the quote is consistent.

options: stolen secretly
bought brand new
taken with permission for temporary use
thrown away
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Borrowed' = taken temporarily with permission, to be returned.

options: The new tenants kept to stop on the landings to look up.
The new tenants kept stop on the landings to look up.
The new tenants have kept stop on the landings to look up.
The new tenants kept stopping on the landings to look up.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'smell of / smell + adverb'.
options: The whole stairwell smelled faintly of fresh paint.
The whole stairwell smell faintly of fresh paint.
The whole stairwell smelled faintly to fresh paint.
The whole stairwell smelled faint of fresh paint.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct preposition: 'standing ___ a borrowed ladder'.
options: in
on
at
by

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence in the negative past simple.
options: Nobody complain, because nobody know whose job it was.
Nobody didn't complained, because nobody didn't know whose job it was.
Nobody complained, because nobody knew whose job it was.
Nobody complained, because nobody don't know whose job it was.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Nobody' is already negative; past simple verbs 'complained' and 'knew' follow normally.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing line.
options: But everyone know who started it.
But everyone knows who start it.
But everyone knowing who started it.
But everyone knows who started it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Everyone' takes third person singular 'knows'; embedded past simple 'started'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does one neighbour painting one wall sometimes change a whole building? What is the difference between waiting for the landlord and just starting?
